findings.description: Direct contradiction: Overview states 'All pipeline stages run in parallel' while Stage 2 states 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins'.
findings.evidence: Overview: 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' Stage 2: 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins.'

findings.description: Dangling reference: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' No Section 6 exists in this document.
findings.evidence: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' Document ends at Conclusions with no Section 6 present.

findings.description: Batch/streaming performance claim is factually inverted. Streaming is more efficient for small files; batch incurs setup overhead that yields no benefit at small sizes.
findings.evidence: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.' Standard data engineering: streaming outperforms batch at small sizes due to batch setup overhead.

findings.description: 99.9% latency reduction claim is implausible and unsupported — no baseline, methodology, or conditions provided.
findings.evidence: 'The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes.' No baseline, conditions, or methodology.

findings.description: Benchmark claim unattributed — 'Benchmarks show' with no source, methodology, or conditions named.
findings.evidence: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.' No source named.

findings.description: 'Pipeline' and 'workflow' used interchangeably and inconsistently — document never settles on which term means what.
findings.evidence: Operational Notes: 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored.' Conclusions: 'a real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline.' No definitions provided.

findings.description: 'Standard retry policy' referenced but never defined — retry count, backoff, and timeout are absent.
findings.evidence: 'Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures.' No definition of retry count, backoff, or timeout.

findings.description: Conclusions reference adding a real-time streaming workflow as a future enhancement, but the document already describes the system as handling both batch and streaming — this reads as a scope contradiction.
findings.evidence: 'Future enhancements include adding a real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline.' Stage 2 already discusses both batch and streaming processing modes.

findings.description: The 5-minute polling interval in Stage 4 contradicts the sub-second latency claim in Stage 3 — monitoring at 5-minute intervals is inconsistent with a system claiming sub-second end-to-end delivery.
findings.evidence: Stage 4: 'The monitoring system polls health endpoints every 5 minutes.' Stage 3: 'reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9%' (implying sub-second delivery).

findings.description: 'Large files' handled differently but threshold is undefined.
findings.evidence: 'Large files are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.' No size threshold specified.

findings.description: ETL acronym not expanded on first use.
findings.evidence: 'an internal ETL system' — ETL not expanded.

findings.description: Authentication type unspecified: 'Authentication is used to verify each request' with no mechanism named.
findings.evidence: 'Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.' No type (API key, OAuth, mTLS) specified.

findings.description: Operational Notes and Stage 4 Monitoring section repeat substantially the same information about the Prometheus dashboard.
findings.evidence: Stage 4: 'The monitoring system uses a Prometheus dashboard.' Operational Notes bullet 1: 'Monitored via the Prometheus dashboard.' Near-duplicate.
